rationale: 6-figure headcount creation directly quantifies budget allocated to solve the intelligence synthesis problem manually — the clearest possible pricing signal.
Gartner, McKinsey, and practitioner research directly quantifying AI ROI failure rates and confirming interpretation complexity as the primary blocker.
Big 4 engagement pricing ($250K–1M+) sets a clear premium pricing anchor and confirms organisations are already paying for the manual equivalent of SCOUT.
Existing spend on inferior tools ($24–60K/year) combined with explicit dissatisfaction and willingness-to-pay language confirms a proven commercial market.
Negative reviews of paid CI tools confirm budget-cleared buyers actively dissatisfied with existing solutions — direct substitution opportunity.
Executive posts describing the board preparation crisis event name the trigger moment explicitly and confirm quarterly recurrence.
Manual synthesis workflows quantify shadow spend ($1,200–2,400/month equivalent) and confirm pre-sold operators ready for runtime substitution.
